heading: Special assistant U.S. attorneys and cross-deputized attorneys
label: 925D Special assistant U.S. attorneys and cross-deputized attorneys
notes: Effective DateSection not effective until Oct. 1 of the first fiscal year beginning after , see , set out as a note under , Commerce and Trade.Mar. 15, 2022section 4(a) of div. W of Pub. L. 117–103section 6851 of Title 15

children.text: appoint, in accordance with , qualified State, Tribal, territorial and local prosecutors and qualified attorneys working for the United States government to serve as special assistant United States attorneys for the purpose of prosecuting violations of such paragraphs; andsection 543 of title 28

deputize State, Tribal, territorial and local law enforcement officers for the purpose of enhancing the capacity of the agents of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives in responding to and investigating violations of such paragraphs.

children.heading: .—Improve Intimate Partner and Public Safety
children.label: b .—Improve Intimate Partner and Public Safety

children.text: identify not fewer than 75 jurisdictions among States, territories and Tribes where there are high rates of firearms violence and threats of firearms violence against intimate partners and other persons protected under paragraphs (8) and (9) of section 922(g) and where local authorities lack the resources to address such violence;
make such appointments as described in subsection (a) in jurisdictions where enhanced enforcement of such paragraphs is necessary to reduce firearms homicide and injury rates; and

children.label: A
children.text: each Field Division of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives; and

children.label: B
children.text: each District Office of the United States Attorneys.

children.heading: .—Qualified Defined
children.label: c .—Qualified Defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified” means, with respect to an attorney, that the attorney is a licensed attorney in good standing with any relevant licensing authority.
